children.text: This project annotates the documentary 'Familia' (2007), as well as Canal-related audiovisual materials and interviews with Río Indio community leader Digna Benite (2022-2025) to examine how documentary and testimonios contest infrastructural projects of water development in contemporary Panama. Focusing on a proposed reservoir project in the Río Indio watershed —first announcement in 1999 and repeatedly revived— the project traces how infrastructural proposals persist across decades while testimonial voices remain continuous, reappearing in both archival and contemporary forms. Through audiovisual annotation, it shows how 'Familia' frames competing narratives of water development, how technocratic discourse justifies infrastructural intervention, and how campesino testimony grounds these abstractions in lived territorial experience.

title: Narrating Waters: Testimony and Infrastructure in the Panama Canal
